title: Podman 4.8.3 stuck at podman machine init on Windows 11
body:
### Issue Description

I installed Podman 4.8.3 on Windows 11 and stuck at podman machine init (I got the same issue with Podman 4.8.2)
I can extract the file fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar.xz manually successfully, not sure why it was failed during the init command.

C:\Users\MyID>podman machine init
Downloading VM image: fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar.xz: done
xz: (stdin): Compressed data is corrupt
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar [--------------] 32.0KiB / 112.0MiB


![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/142728322/998f655e-2ab4-4f8a-9ddc-b00415ceb974)
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/142728322/37cee7de-9b75-4945-ba77-c64eee1cc119)




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Installed podman 4.8.3 from https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.8.3/podman-4.8.3-setup.exe (wsl option checked)
2. Run podman machine init



### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:

Podman machine init command was failed to execute.

C:\Users\MyID>podman machine init
Downloading VM image: fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar.xz: done
xz: (stdin): Compressed data is corrupt
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar [--------------] 32.0KiB / 112.0MiB

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected:
Podman machine init command is successful.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version: 4.8.3
operating system: Windows 11 64 bit (Intel)

C:\Users\MyID>podman machine init
Downloading VM image: fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar.xz: done
xz: (stdin): Compressed data is corrupt
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.16.tar [--------------] 32.0KiB / 112.0MiB
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Can you paste the output of

`where xz` (assuming you are using cmd prompt like above, `(Get-Command xz.exe).Path` if you are using powershell
and 
`xz --version`


I suspect whats happening is you have an older version of XZ utils installed. We prefer to use a system xz utils if it's around, and fallback to a local internal implementation if not.

I had the same problem here, even when I set --log-level=debug it still stuck at 16.4Mib and I got no error message at all
![Screenshot 2024-02-29 004016](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/78090778/f301b6a1-803c-43a1-bc23-c174119fc805)
@SanHo09 this looks like a different underlying than the OP, but just for completeness can you post the above asks in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21187#issuecomment-1955358347

Hi @n1hility, 
I'm new to Podman, and I don't really know about the xz you're talking about.

I simply downloaded Podman 4.9.3 and ran **podman machine init**, and it stuck at the line "_Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.47.tar [=>------- ------] 16.4MiB / 112.4MiB_" and didn't give me any other errors. The loading simply stopped.
Do you have any advice on debugging or viewing the logs?
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/78090778/744773f1-1136-46ca-add7-45735e3c6a83)

Update: I tried downloading and running Podman on my wsl and it works perfectly fine, but on Windows it doesn't :(
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/78090778/abef104b-70e8-4ccb-8985-f127735259df)

It looks like .file: **podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.48** caught some errors on the first initialization, so I went to the path **C:/Users/your username/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/wsl** and deleted the error .tar file.

Init podman machine again and it will run smoothly

Hi @n1hility, Here is the xz result (I just tried with latest Podman 4.9.2 as of 2024-03-08 and still have the same issue)

Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.22631.3155]
(c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.

C:\Users\MyID>podman machine init
xz: (stdin): Compressed data is corrupt
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v39.0.50.tar [--------------] 64.0KiB / 112.5MiB

C:\Users\MyID> xz --version
xz (XZ Utils) 5.2.3
liblzma 5.2.3

C:\Users\MyID>podman --version
podman version 4.9.2
I tried to delete the tar file as @SanHo09 mentioned above but it did not help.
I tried the commands in Cygwin, it ran successfully. The problem happens with Windows console or Powershell.
AFAIK podman 5.0 switch to zstd for the os images so I assume this is fixed.
